mappings.rationale: The primary VSI for this section, directly covering growth, differentiation, morphogenesis, embryo formation, and organ development in both plants and animals.
Directly addresses cell differentiation, development in vitro (cell and tissue cultures), and the potential for regeneration.
Covers the cell cycle, cell growth, cell division, and fertilization—the constituent cellular processes underlying biological development.
Covers plant development including pollination, fertilization, embryo formation, seed germination, and the sporophyte plant body.
Addresses the genetic control and regulation of development including gene expression patterns during differentiation.
Directly relevant to aging and decline in animals, including senescence, causes of aging, and the duration of life.
Covers the molecular mechanisms of gene regulation underlying the control and integration of developmental processes.
Addresses hormonal control of development including growth hormones and the hormonal regulation of metamorphosis and maturation.
Relevant to biological malformation and abnormal development, where the control of cell growth and differentiation goes awry.
Provides context for organ formation and the development of organ systems in vertebrates.
